Title: Identification of plant genes putatively involved in the perception of fungal ergosterol-squalene

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LRR domain-containing receptor-like protein; probable LRR receptor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with plant perception/response to fungal ergosterol-squalene
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tomato homolog XP_004250531.1 was among the most strongly upregulated LRR-domain genes after ergosterol/squalene treatment (6.37-fold). 2. Arabidopsis mutant α666C carrying a homologous-gene disruption showed altered response to ergosterol/squalene, including strong AOC upregulation. 3. The gene is therefore implicated in perception/response to fungal ergosterol-squalene, but decisive nutrient-metabolism control was not established.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tomato RNA-seq; Arabidopsis mutant phenotyping and RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in tomato roots grown with ergosterol/squalene at T34 concentrations
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fungal membrane sterol/intermediate acting as MAMP-like signals in plant-fungus interaction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ergosterol plus squalene at concentrations corresponding to Trichoderma harzianum T34; comparison with Botrytis cinerea-related assays in Arabidopsis mutant screen
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate receptor-like gene for engineering plant responsiveness to beneficial fungi, but direct utility remains unvalidated
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The specific Arabidopsis gene name was not provided in the text; classified as Common Gene because it is a putative receptor/perception component, not an enzyme or TF.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Tomato RNA-seq using Ion Proton platform; accession NA

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: G-type lectin S-receptor; LRR/PRR-related receptor-like protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation altered Arabidopsis response to ergosterol/squalene, including reduced aerial growth under treatment and strong upregulation of AOC, NPR1, and BIK1.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tomato gene encoding XP_004238856.2 was highly upregulated by ergosterol/squalene (4.05-fold). 2. Arabidopsis homolog mutant α987C showed altered transcriptomic response to ergosterol/squalene, with strong induction of AOC and PR genes NPR1 and BIK1. 3. The gene likely participates in plant perception/signaling triggered by fungal ergosterol-squalene.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tomato RNA-seq; Arabidopsis homolog mutant analysis; RT-qPCR of marker genes; phenotype comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in tomato roots grown with ergosterol/squalene at T34 concentrations
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fungal membrane sterol/intermediate acting as MAMP-like signals
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ergosterol plus squalene at T34 concentrations
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate receptor-mediated plant responses to beneficial vs quasi-pathogenic fungi; currently exploratory
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation caused reduced aerial growth in presence of ergosterol/squalene
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific Arabidopsis locus not stated in text; receptor-like class inferred from tomato protein annotation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Tomato RNA-seq using Ion Proton platform; accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor family; WRKY40-like DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fungal membrane molecules acting as MAMP-like cues during fungus-plant interactions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Hormone-related defense metabolism, especially salicylic acid and jasmonate/ethylene signaling/biosynthetic response to ergosterol-squalene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; represses SA-related genes and positively regulates part of JA/ET-related genes in response to ergosterol/squalene
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL; PBS3; ICS; EPS1; JAR; AOC; ACS; ACO
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates SA-pathway genes (PAL, ICS, EPS1; also PBS3/EPS1 elevated in mutant) and positively regulates JA/ET-related genes including JAR, AOC, and ACS under ergosterol/squalene-associated conditions; influences ACO behavior during Botrytis infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of WRKY40-like function causes stronger aerial and root development in the presence of ergosterol/squalene and shifts defense metabolism toward SA-associated gene induction while reducing JA/ET-associated responses.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator identified by mutant analysis; the α331 mutant displayed the strongest phenotype and transcriptomic deviation, establishing WRKY40 as the principal regulator of plant response to fungal ergosterol/squalene in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Exogenous ergosterol and squalene at Trichoderma T34-like concentrations; Botrytis cinerea infection context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Tomato WRKY40-like NP_001304843.1 strongly upregulated in roots by ergosterol/squalene (8.63-fold)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred functional activity during Arabidopsis response to ergosterol/squalene and Botrytis infection; transcript level of mutant gene not applicable
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ergosterol plus squalene at T34 concentrations; Botrytis cinerea B05.10 infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tomato RNA-seq; Arabidopsis homolog knockout mutant analysis; RT-qPCR of SA/JA/ET/PR/development genes; pathogen challenge assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tomato WRKY40-like gene NP_001304843.1 was among the most strongly induced transcripts after ergosterol/squalene exposure. 2. Arabidopsis mutant α331 lacking the homolog showed the most pronounced developmental and transcriptional changes under ergosterol/squalene treatment. 3. In α331, SA biosynthetic genes PBS3 and EPS1 were strongly upregulated, while JA/ET-related genes were broadly downregulated relative to wild type. 4. During Botrytis cinerea infection with ergosterol/squalene, the mutant showed increased PAL, ICS, EPS1, and ACO but reduced JAR, AOC, and ACS versus wild type. 5. The paper concludes that WRKY40 negatively regulates SA-related genes and positively regulates ethylene/jasmonate-related genes in response to fungal ergosterol/squalene.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory target for tuning plant responses to fungal sterol cues and balancing beneficial-fungus colonization versus defense activation; manipulation may alter SA-JA/ET tradeoff and pathogen responses, so deployment would require caution.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function shifts defense balance toward SA and away from JA/ET, which may alter resistance spectrum and interactions with beneficial fungi/pathogens.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No Arabidopsis homolog was identified for the second tomato WRKY40-like gene XP_004245210.1, so only NP_001304843.1 homolog was functionally pursued. The study frames WRKY40 as activated downstream of perception of ergosterol/squalene rather than as the receptor itself.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Tomato RNA-seq using Ion Proton platform; accession NA
